title: Part 2
text: security and the manager’s office key. ▶The shop contains a U-shaped counter filled with weapons from all eras of history. All are real and fully usable. ▶There are standard weapons (sword, ax, mace, bow, daggers). but Emmy thinks those are boring. ▶Her store contains specially curated weapons such as: 15. EMMY’S SWORDS & STUFF d10WEAPONS 01Macuahuitl 02Katar 03Zhua 04Atlatl & Spears 05Urumi 06Bagh Nakh Emmy’s most prized weapons hang on wall 07A Three-Section Staff 08Tiger Fork 09Claw Hand 10Guillotine Hat (with chain) Level 3 // A goldmine for clues, whisper cards, and horror. Use them to build paranoia. [19] Cabinets: Filing cabinets tightly jammed together line the walls. ▶ Unlocked Cabinet: Old files, 3 bottles of liquor, a folded poncho and a bundle of chemical flares. ▶ Locked Cabinet: Easily broken into, it contains a medium-sized ornate wooden box. �Opening the box sends a puff of spores into the face of anyone close by - Whisper Card (p. 37). �Inside are a pair of human lungs, connected to varied growths by fungal webbing. The lungs slowly inflate and deflate. CCTVs: Each cabinet has an old CRT TV and a small clicker remote which cycles through security feeds. ▶An image of a steel door with tracks of mud below - (Clue). ▶ A Child of Ammon (p. 9). �Either a quick glimpse or a visible NPC being REPLACED - Whisper Card (p. 37). ▶ Different factions starting to rise up: �Maggie (p. 6) breaking cameras near her office. �Griffith (p. 7) gathering people into the fold, handing out food, weapons and tracksuits. Small waiting area with single wooden door leads to back room. ▶A cramped office with mud tracks throughout. ▶ Chair: An odd fuzz covers the rolling chair, its tendrils waving in the still office. Touching it triggers a Whisper Card (p. 37). ▶Old wooden desk with peeling varnish is in the center. Locked Main Drawer. ▶A loaded revolver, bullets (12). ▶Alphabetical list of mall employees & frequent patrons. Reading it grants a Whisper Card (p. 37). �Lists place of employment, birthdate and ‘viability’ from 1-10. �A select few have an odd symbol drawn next to their name (red triangle intersected by a spiral). Locked Side Drawer. ▶A packed notebook. Reading it triggers a Whisper Card (p. 37). �Runic shapes surrounding a diagram of a flesh hole. ▶A supply list: lamp, pickaxe, rope, shovel, cart. ▶Polaroids of multiple people. �Some contain the missing people. �Each picture person bound, eyes completely white, being fed a fleshy, bulbous object. �Looking at the pictures triggers a Whisper Card (p. 37). 16. THE MANAGER’S OFFICE LEVEL 3 FOOD COURT HELP DESK STAN’S PRETZELS MANAGER’S OFFICE JOE’S BURGER SPIRAL CINEMA [20] Level 3 // Kurt’s name was a very intentional choice. What could be in his bug out bag? ▶Filled with red plastic tabletops and white chairs. ▶Multiple food shops surround the space, many long since closed. 17. FOOD COURT ▶Standard three-screen cinema, with knock off concession stand. ▶Currently playing The Thing (1983), The Being (1983), The Deadly Spawn (1983). 20. SPIRAL CINEMA The windows are adorned large-scale posters line the walls, featuring extreme closeups of people eating burgers and fries. ▶Poorly locked gate. ▶The till is open, still full of cash. ▶Ketchup bottles are midway through being refilled. ▶The main container fell to the floor, splattering the walk-in door with a high-pressure burst of red. �Scratch marks line the ketchup, as though someone were dragged away. Two broken fingernails were left behind - Whisper Card (p. 37). 19. JOE’S BURGER BARN A pastel pink pretzel shop devoted to all things pretzels. ▶A picture of Kurt(p. 8) hangs in the employee of the month placard. ▶Over 100 homemade dipping sauces and toppings. ▶Kurt’s bug out bag is hidden under the front counter. 18. STAN’S PRETZEL STAND Basement and Lower Tunnels // "Abandon all hope, ye who enter here." [21] BASEMENT AND LOWER TUNNELS [22] Basement // Lots of reasons to come down here (fix electricity, clues about the mines, Mall Rats). Under the mall is a mess of hallways and forgotten storage rooms. A nightmare to navigate, even before the crisis, many of these areas were lost to time and changes in management. HALLWAYS ▶Signs of abandonment (dust and cobwebs). �Closer inspection reveals an inordinate amount of heavy traffic (scuffs, dirt, things pushed to clear more space, dark stains). �All the tracks lead to the Bunker (p. 23). UNMARKED STORAGE ROOMS ▶Large, full of old mall furniture and fixtures. ▶Connects the Access Hallways (p. 11) to each floor, including the basement. ▶Florescent light flickers incessantly. ▶Specks of blood, mud, and mold stain the floor of the elevator. ▶Some bloody fingernails are embedded in the tattered padding along the walls. 21A. FREIGHT ELEVATOR Failing lights that fade the closer toward the basement you go. ▶Oppressive concrete with graffiti filling the stairwell near the basement. ▶There is vent access sporadically throughout. ▶Double doors open into the basement. These frequently jam and require effort to open. 21B. MAINTENANCE STAIRWELL Right off the basement’s main entrance, a pair of large steel doors lead to the backup generators and water pumps. ▶Two massive generators fill the main room, with utility cables webbing out and up. ▶Large chrome fuel tanks line the back wall, tubes connecting them to the generators. ▶The connecting room pumps water from an underground well, all the way up to rooftop water towers. 22. UTILITY ROOM Located in a long-abandoned storeroom (with improvised locking mechanism making entrance from the basement hallways difficult). ▶Old TV and game system, surrounded by ad-hoc furniture. ▶There is vent access from inside. This is the main way in and out of the clubhouse. ▶The higher the DOOM Level, the more high-quality supplies are being stored here. ▶50% chance a wounded Mall Rat is being tended to by their friend. A large amount of blood pools around them. 23. MALL RATS CLUBHOUSE BASEMENT The Bunker & Below // Here’s where things become most dungeon-like. Survival is far from guaranteed. [23] THE BUNKER AND WHAT LAY BELOW The Mall was built over an abandoned mine. The opening created in the bunker connects the basement to the once dormant mining tunnels. ▶The tunnels are dark, with light sources being required. The walls are hewn from stone and dirt. ▶Old wood reinforces the pathways. ▶A duffle bag containing zip ties, rope, and a Polaroid camera is stashed in a small alcove here. ▶The passageways vary in size and skill in construction, weaving alongside natural formations. Paths branching off into darkness. Three pathways connect to: ▶B. Tunnel of Teeth ▶C. Replaced ▶E. The Tangle (p. 24) A. ENTRY TO THE MINES The path turns to reveal a tight passage lined with jagged teeth [Save or d4 damage]. Three pathways connect to: ▶A. Entry to the Mines ▶E. The Tangle (p. 24) ▶G. Movement in the Darkness (p. 24) B. TUNNEL OF TEETH The sound of persistent wet squishing echos prior to their arrival. The tunnel is completely covered in eyes. They vary in color and shape. All look at those who approach. Three pathways connect to: ▶C. Replaced ▶E. The Tangle (p. 24) ▶H. Ventilation Access (p. 24) D. TUNNEL OF EYES In the far reaches of the sub- basements hang a massive reinforced set of double doors. ▶A padlock clasps these doors shut. ▶Keys are found: �In Billy’s Office(p. 14). �On Mr. Neil’s (p. 6). �The lock can be broken with tools or force, but failing can cause bodily harm, break the tool or alert others to the party’s presence. ▶Inside is an underground shelter with everything a small group (up to 10) would need to survive. ▶The bunks against the back wall have been torn out and cast aside. ▶A crack in the wall has been widened into a large opening, revealing a tunnel leading into the damp, dark earth. ▶Cans of gasoline, two headlamps, a pickax lay beside the opening. 24. THE BUNKER A REPLACED NPC (p. 8) lays here, unconscious. If awoken they will ask the party for help. Three pathways connect to: ▶A. Entry to the Mines ▶D. Tunnel of Eyes ▶E. The Tangle (p. 24) C. REPLACED [24] The Mines // The Tangle is the central hub down here. F. MEMORY GARDEN The tunnel branches off, narrowing and rising until it is a vertical shaft. An old ragged rope and piton are set into its side, allowing access to a vent grate and the mall’s ventilation system (p. 13). Four pathways connect to: ▶4. The Vents (p. 13) ▶D. Tunnel of Eyes (p. 23) ▶E. The Tangle ▶L. Taken (p. 25) H. VENTILATION ACCESS A mess of weaving passageways and tunnels, often turning around and leading back on themselves. A hub in which it is easy to get lost within. Nine pathways of note connect to The Tangle: ▶A. Entry to the Mines (p. 23) ▶B. Tunnel of Teeth (p. 23) ▶C. Replaced (p. 23) ▶D. Tunnel of Eyes (p. 23) ▶G. Movement in the Darkness ▶H. Ventilation Access ▶I. Fruiting Body ▶J. Child of Ammon ▶L. Taken (p. 25) E. THE TANGLE The party is being hunted through the tunnels. Successful DEX keeps them safe, but any failure will result in taking damage, losing an item or becoming separated. Three pathways connect to: ▶B. Tunnel of Teeth (p. 23) ▶E. The Tangle ▶F. The Memory Garden G. MOVEMENT IN THE DARKNESS The space bends and changes, reenacting a warped memory made of flesh. ▶The space attempts to isolate the party and keep them here. ▶A randomly chosen player gets a Whisper Card (p. 37), as a pivotal moment from their PC’s past plays out around them, in a warped, funhouse style. Two pathways connect to: ▶G. Movement in the Darkness ▶N. The Portal (p. 27) Stagnant spores around a Fruiting Body (p. 13). ▶The leading party member - Whisper Card (p. 37). ▶Others must make save/plan to avoid the spores or get a Whisper Card (p. 37). Four pathways connect to: ▶F. The Memory Garden ▶G. Movement in the Darkness ▶E. The Tangle ▶M. The Fungal Fields (p. 26) I. FRUITING BODY Mid-transformation, part of an NPC the party has met previously is visible in the mass of flesh. Three pathways connect to: ▶E. The Tangle ▶K. Mine Tunnel (p. 25) ▶M. The Fungal Fields (p. 26) J. CHILD OF AMMON The Mines // Being separated can lead to fate far worse than death. [25] A branching path. To the right, a faint shadow of a person beckons the PCs. Each PC must make a CTRL save. ▶ Success: The shadow doesn’t look or sound right (1 Stress). ▶ Failure: The person they love most beckons them down the right path - Whisper Card (p. 37): �Characters who follow reach a dead end with a narrow Hole in the floor. �Eyes peer out, as the “person” begs them to enter. Entering the Hole: The first PC that enters crawls a ways until they come to a tight bend. A hand reaches out to them. ▶ If they take it: They crawl onward, through darkness and lose communication with the party. They will reappear later, in the Fungal Fields (p. 26). �They have been REPLACED. Hand them the blank Whisper Card. ▶ If they refuse: Reveal it was actually a dead end. Allow them to crawl out. ▶Additional players who enter reach an impassable choke point and need help exiting - Whisper Card (p. 37). L. TAKEN An exposed vein above a stash of old mining equipment. Includes an expired box of TNT. Four pathways connect to: ▶J. Child of Ammon (p. 24) ▶L. Taken (p. 25) ▶M. The Fungal Fields (p. 26) ▶N. The Portal (p. 27) K. MINE TUNNEL SEPARATED Navigating dark spaces always carries the risk of getting separated. Use these rules for being separated or escaping the mines. ▶Being separated triggers a Whisper Card (p. 37). ▶Pass a CTRL save to be reunited, if you are separated. ▶Failure can result in: �Being attacked. �Finding an item from your past (Stress). �Finding a bloody item that belongs to another PC. �Losing an item. �Hearing another PC saying a prayer to the “Pure Flesh” (Stress). [26] Fungal Fields // Ammon’s corruption made into a beautific field of fleshy stalks. Stalks of bioluminescent fungus and flesh, 6-8 feet high, fill the cavernous space. ▶Each time a PC enters a fungal field they receive a Whisper Card (p. 34). ▶As the DOOMClock progresses, fields of fungi can appear throughout the mall (sprouting from spores). ▶Across the cavern, a perfectly carved archway pulses with red light. ▶A fungal panther uses the fields as its hunting grounds. Roll d10 on the Navigate the Fungal Fields table. ▶Reroll repeated results. M. FUNGAL FIELDS d10 NAVIGATING THE FUNGAL FIELDS 01 The party gets SEPARATED (p. 23). 02 Stalks of elongated hands reach and grab at you. 03 Every inch of the space is filled with different types of eyes, watching your every move (Stress). 04 One of the stalks seems to have a face. Whoever examines closely finds it to be the face of the person they love (50%) or hate (50%) the most - Whisper Card (p. 37). 05 The stalks behind you jerk, as though something large is coming your way. 06 Surprise attack by the Fungal Panther (p. 24)! It attacks, then runs back into the field. 07 Encounter a Fruiting Body (p. 13) - the party leader gets a Whisper Card (p. 37). 08 An NPC, clothes tattered, crying. 09Endless stalks slowly waving. 10 You make it out the other side, at an opening that leads to the Portal (p. 10). FUNGAL PANTHER STR: 10 DEX: 14 CTRL: 13 HP: 8 Armor: 1 Bite (d8), Tail (d6), Claws (d8+d8) ▶Four multi-jointed limbs ending in razor sharp spurs of bone. Flesh curling back in ribbons. ▶Once was human. ▶The spine has stretched into a boney, prehensile, whip-like appendage extending from the base of its neck. ▶Jagged “fangs” line its multi-hinged mandibles. The Portal // Not everyone needs to be a hero. Sometimes you just need to be the villain. [27] A cavernous space, something dark and primal pulsing within. ▶ If the party came from the Fungal Fields, a small pathway leads up to the ledge, in front of the cavernous room containing the portal. ▶ If the party came from the tunnels (F or K) they arrive on a ledge leading toward the opening. Off to the side is a seemingly endless field of swaying fungalstalks. THE WOMB: This vast expanse is illuminated by the “object” that fills its center. ▶A massive gate of flesh, bone and tumorous fungus. ▶The object bubbles and pulses as it slowly grows and expands. Put the final card face up on the table and read it to the players. THE PORTAL IS OPENING With enough time and organic material, it willtear itself in such a way that it can never be closed. ▶Reality is thin in this space. ▶What is beyond is unclear, but the smell and colors spilling forth are almost overwhelming. ▶With the right toolsand effort, the gate can be destroyed. ▶Players can walk through the portal, but will be unable to return from beyond its arches. N. THE PORTAL As the party approaches the Portal, place this card in the center of the table. ▶ If you have not been REPLACED: You feel an immense sense of dread and wrongness when looking upon this portal. ▶ If you have been REPLACED: Transform and protect the portal. CHILDREN OF AMMON Add d4 to each stat (STR/DEX/CTRL/HP) ▶Damage: d6 - d12 (number of appendages, size, bones, fungus). ▶Metamorphosis: Can change forms in moments, sometimes even breaking off into multiple unique instances. ▶Anointed Blood: Spray a mix of blood, and spores. Witnesses take d4 Stress. ▶Critical Damage: Fleshy tendrils pierce the victim’s flesh, worming their way between muscle and bone (D4 damage to DEX). L E [28] Encounters // While there isn’t an explicit encounter roll mechanic, use these to amp up the tension. ENCOUNTERS Use this tiered encounter table to randomly generate events throughout The Mall. Since the Doom Clock changes in response to player intervention, use it to guide the severity of the encounter. ▶Many of these encounters may trigger a Whisper Card (p. 37). d10TIER 1 (DOOM Clock 3-5) 01 A Mall Rat (p. 7) shouts for help! Their friend fell and got trapped in the vent (near a Fruiting Body p. 13). 02An NPC clutching their stomach complaining about pain. 03A mangled name tag (30% chance it’s readable). 04Someone attempts to break through the barrier. 05Beth the Mall Rat (p. 7), searching for supplies. 06Griffith (p. 7) or Maggie (p. 6) giving a speech. 07Movement in the vents. 08A trail of blood leading to a random shop (p. 5). 09 A band of NPCs surrounding someone, accusing them of being infected. 10A Child of Ammon attacks (p. 9). d10TIER 2 (DOOM Clock 6-8) 01 An NPC proclaims, “It dwells below our feet!” right before they jump from the topmost level. 02Someone attempting to "cut it out." 03 A fleshless “Angel” (p. 9) attacks from the shadows, attempting to subdue and consume. 04A fire breaks out in a random shop (p. 5). 05A large cloud of spores drifts through the mall. 06Beth (p. 7) offering a safe space to a blood-covered NPC. 07Open physical conflict between two factions. 08Recent human remains, with large pieces missing. 09 A Child Of Ammon in the form of a rabbit-parakeet-kitten chimera goes on a rampage (p. 9). 10A band of NPCs killing someone accused of being infected. TIER 3 Encounters // DOOM is all but certain. Can the PCs die on their own terms? [29] d4TIER 3 (DOOM Clock 9+) 01 The ground shudders as a sinkhole consumes the roller rink, leading directly to the Fungal Fields (p. 26) below the mall. 02A pack of Fungal Panthers (p. 26) hunt through the mall. 03People on the “hunt.” 04The culling begins. ENCOUNTERSB PERSONNEL 1. Mr. Neil - Mall Manager (p. 6) 2. Billy - direct report to Mr. Neil (p. 6) 3. Kurt - Stan’s Pretzel Stand (p. 8) 4. Maggie - Head of the CBB (p. 6) 5. Griffith -Your Body is a Temple (p. 7) 6. Samuel - Griffith’s right hand (p. 7) 7. William- Head mall walker (p. 6) 8. Bruce - Maintenance (p. 8) 9. Terry - Night Security (p. 8) 10. Hudson - Head of Security (p. 7) 11. Beth - Head of the Mall Rats (p. 7) 12. Ruth - Patron (p. 7) 13. Emmy - Emmy’s Swords & Stuff (p. 8) 14. Logan - DMV Express (p. 8) Level 1 1. Contest Stage (p. 12) 2. Outside the Mall (p. 12) 3. Parking Garages - all levels (p. 13) 4. Vents - all levels (p. 13) 5. Maintenance (p. 14) 6. Loading Docks (p. 14) 7. Your Body is a Temple (p. 14) 8. Polly’s Pet Emporium (p. 15) 9. DMV Express (p. 15) 10. Skate World (p. 15) 11. Mootsy’s Arcade (p. 15) Level 2 12. Break Room (p. 16) 13. Security (p. 17) 14. Business Association (p. 18) 15. Emmy’s Swords & Stuff (p. 18) Level 3 16. The Manager’s Office (p. 19) 17. Food Court (p. 20) 18. Stan’s Pretzel Stand (p. 20) 19. Joe’s Burger Barn (p. 20) 20. Spiral Cinema (p. 20) Basement 22. Utility Room (p. 22) 23. Mall Rats Clubhouse (p. 22) 24. The Bunker (p. 23) [30] Fallouts // The weirder you get, the more others may think you have been REPLACED. CUSTOM FALLOUT These new fallouts reinforce the mall’s "weird" happenings. They can be used instead of or in addition to the core Fallout Table in LH Investigators. ▶When a player fails a CTRL save due to stress, give them a Whisper Card (p. 37) and roll or choose a Fallout. ▶Keep the actual Fallout results secret from other players. d8FALLOUT TABLE 01 You hear the choir's song, its melody is an ever-changing constant. ▶Increase your CTRL by 1d4. 02 Rough patches have formed on your body—light red, surrounding a deep green. Are they getting bigger? ▶Increase your HP by d4. 03 Your insides feel like they are shifting, changing. Bones and joints start to move in ways they couldn't before. The changes are concealed by your clothing... for now. ▶Increase your STR by 1d6. 04 Thoughts and feelings become a thing you can sense, even when not in their proximity. Focus, hard, and words and phrases will enter your mind (onlooking PCs gain 1 Stress). ▶The more you focus on others, the more you lose yourself. 05 You continually see a figure out of the corner of your eye. It regularly shifts and warps, sometimes in horrific ways. You think there's a pattern to the change. 06 Piercing vision. With enough concentration you can see echoes of possible futures (onlooking PCs gain 1 Stress). Doing so changes your eyes. ▶Once per session have the Facilitator tell you a vision. 07 Sounds begin to manifest visually. At first, they are just shapes and colors, but they slowly start to become more distinct. Their form is unlike anything you've ever seen. 08Roll on the Liminal Horror Investigators Fallout Table. Nightmares // Everyone needs to sleep eventually. Proximity to the Portal means it offers little respite. [31] NIGHTMARES Whenever characters sleep, have them make a CTRL save. ▶ Success: The player cannot remember their dreams. ▶ Failure: roll d12 on the nightmare table: d12NIGHTMARE TABLE 01 Something moves underneath your skin, if only you could get a hold of it. 02 You stand before a bubbling portal. A chorus of voices is calling you home. 03 You are surrounded on all sides by stone and dirt, allowing only a small bit of movement. Something sharp starts biting into your feet. 04 The scent of cooking flesh makes your mouth water. It is time to tend the meat on the spit. You feel a small pain where your arm once was. 05 Their smile brings a warmth to your chest as they interlace their fingers in yours. Flames embrace you both. A pool of rendered fat sizzles at your feet. 06Limbs erupt out of your back and begin to pull you apart. 07 A pile of pink lays on the table before you. One more strip of flesh left until it is complete. 08He reaches within you. Bones snap as he builds something new. 09 It is almost finished growing within you. You are running out of time to make it home. 10 Fungus erupts from your every opening. You try to scream, but only yellow spores spill out. 11 As you take another bite, thick viscous fluid spills down your chin. Each mouthful catches in your throat as it wriggles its way deep within you. 12 You are falling deep within the earth, mud and stone packed tight around you gives way to pulsing flesh and bone. Finally you land in front of a massive beating heart that unfurls itself, exposing an unending abyss full of stars. [32] Character Creation // Using this process makes characters with ties to the mall and its denizens. Alternative character creation rules for The Mall, for use in place of Liminal Horror Investigators: Creating an Investigator. ▶A digital version can be found on the Liminal Horror Websiteor here: Each person at the table will propose one shop to be added to the mall (this is not necessarily their PC’s place of work). ▶State its name. ▶Description. ▶Floor (1-3). STEP 1: ADDING TO THE MALL CHARACTER CREATION: THE MALL Roll 3d6 for each of your ability scores (any two results may be swapped): ▶ Strength (STR): Physicality, brawn, toughness ▶ Dexterity (DEX): Speed, sneaking, precision ▶ Control (CTRL): Willpower, charm, weird Roll 1d6 for Hit Protection (HP). STEP 2: ABILITIES Answer the following questions: STEP 3: CHARACTER DETAILS WHY ARE YOU AT THE MALL? (likely reasons) You work at the mall. Where do you work? (p. 5) You are a customer. What is your reason for visiting today? You and your friends hang out here. What “scene” are you a part of? d12 HOW DOES YOUR CHARACTER FEEL ABOUT THE MALL? 01Things are looking up. 02 People are starting to notice the effort you are putting in. 03Your talent is being wasted. 04Is my shift over yet? 05 This place is the root of your problems. 06You cannot escape this place. 07 Some of the people you care about most work here. 08 The other people at the mall are the worst. 09When you’re here, you’re family. 10A job’s a job. 11 Things are going to turn around soon. 12This place is your prison. Character Creation // Once the barrier goes up, money holds little worth. [33] ▶Choose 2 items your character brought to the mall (or roll once on each table). ▶Employee lockers are in the Break Room (p. 16). Employees mark what item(s) are there at the start of play. ▶Most mall visitors do not have weapons larger than a knife (d6) or taser/mace (DEX save or momentarily stunned). The table below provides examples. More are included in the core rules. STEP 4: STARTING GEAR d20EQUIPMENT TABLE 1 01Leather Jacket 02Spray Paint Cans 03Polaroid Camera 04Skateboard 05Roller Skates 06Boombox 07Six-Pack 08Gum 09Lighter 10Nail Polish Remover 11Paint Pens 12Lock Pick Set 13Purchase From Swords & Stuff 14Cosplay 15Scene-Appropriate Attire 16Pack of Cigarettes 17Taser (d6 targets DEX) 18DIY Tattoo Kit 19Pet 20Instrument d20EQUIPMENT TABLE 2 01Knife (d6) 02Music Player & Headphones 03Mouth Spray 04Workout Equipment 05Remote Control Car 06Key Wallet 07JNCO Jeans 08Walkie Talkies 09Tool(s) 10Handcuffs 11Bundle of Nylon Rope 12Letter of Resignation 13A Fifth 14Shirt, Tie, Slacks 15Shoplifted Item 16Civilian Clothes 17Lacrosse Stick 18Effigy 19Stolen key to a shop 20Gun (d6) [34] Character Creation // Making NPCs leads to the best moments in play. ▶ Age: Choose or roll 1d12+17. ▶ Look: Choose a style of clothing or look for your character. �Employees: What’s your uniform? Did you bring a change of clothes? ▶ Name: What is your character’s name? STEP 5: GENERAL INFO What rumor did your PC hear recently? STEP 7: RUMORS STOP! This step is completed as a party/table. CONNECTIONS Choose/create an NPC that your character has a connection with. ▶Describe them and their connection to you. BONDS Describe the bond your PC has with another PC. STEP 6: ENTANGLEMENTS d20 EXAMPLE BONDS 01Crush11Lent Money 02Dating12Annoyed by 03 Ex (friend or lover) 13 Helped out of a bind. 04 Work Proximity Associate 14 Wish Was Friend 05 Obsessed With 15Parental Figure 06Rival16Mentor 07 (Vice) Buddy 17Work Spouse 08Confidant18Co-Worker 09Enemy19Supervisor 10Frenemy20Friend d12RUMORS 01 The disappearances in town have something to do with the mall. 02 Terry has a bunch of contraband hidden in security. 03 If you can get into the vents, you can get anywhere in the mall. 04 Billy (Mr. Neil’s assistant) is living off of the loading dock 05 Mr. Neil has been tracking mud all over the mall. 06 There are hidden cameras in the mall. 07Maggie carries a gun. 08 The Mall Rats sneak into the mall at night. 09Emmy's weapons are real. 10 Joe’s Burger Barn is closed today because Joe’s being investigated. Change This Module // Nothing is sacred here, change it to fit your needs! [35] WAYS TO CHANGE THIS MODULE ▶Directly connect Your Body Is a Temple (p. 14) to the weird. They chose this mall based on the same type of research Mr. Neil was doing. They want to harness the thing and its portal to bring about the reign of their own "god." ▶Introduce an NPC directly connected to the weird. Perhaps a cynical, heavy-smoking magic- wielding detective who came to investigate the temporal shifts beneath the mall. ▶Increase (or decrease) the number of “You've Been REPLACED” Whisper Card (p. 37). ▶Make The REPLACED(p. 8) more distinct in their odd behavior (introducing Invasion of the Body Snatchers as a touchstone). ▶Increase the population of the mall and lean heavily into faction play. ▶Introduce well-equipped Bureau Agents who will prioritize containing or extracting any paranatural assets, with a secondary mission to keep knowledge of this event from spreading. AGENTS OF THE O.D.D. Framing The O.D.D. has intel that an oddity is being used in a highly populated location. It requires immediate containment. Agents have been dispatched to extract the Arcana and ensure a quick solution to the ever evolving situation. Ability Scores CTRL can be REPLACED directly with WIL. Embracing Horror Whisper Cards can be used as an optional mechanic, but if the table does not want to have Agents run the risk of being REPLACED, instead lean heavily into Liminal Horror’s inventory and Fallout systems. THE COMPANY Framing The WBC has sent agents to recruit (or neutralize) potential employee #758B (Logan D of the Carpenter DMV). On route they received word of a potential Asset being deployed at the same mall. The Asset Recovery and Containment Division (ARC) is now tasked with recovery of any potential resources. Standard information suppression protocol is also in place. Stress Translates directly. Armor & Stability Armor or Stability instead adds to BODY. Fallout If a character rolls on the Burnout Effects they also receive a Fallout (p.x). Fallout Stat increases map to the skill that makes the most sense. Enemy Conversion: ▶ Health: A Variable’s Health equals HP divide by 2 ▶ Aware: A Variable’s Aware is 1 if DEX is 1-10 2 if DEX is11-14, 3 if DEX is 15+. ▶ Combat: A Variable’s Combat is based on damage dice: 1(d4), 2(d6), 3(d8), 4(d10+) The following conversion guide advises on how to use this module with systems other than Liminal Horror. In play, use the chosen system’s character generation, rules and equipment stats. CONVERSION GUIDES [36] Conversion Guides // Liminal Horror adventures can adapt to a myriad of systems. MONSTER OF THE WEEK Framing There is something brewing at The Carpenter Mall. You’ve never been here personally, but a reliable contact swears by it. People have been
